narrative.headline: AUKUS submarine weapons integration accelerates with $1B DSCA notification
narrative.body: A $1B DSCA case covers UK-specific vertical launch tubes and common weapon launchers for AUKUS submarines, preceded by a cluster of UK-tagged lobbying filings spanning AI risk, patents, and FY27 defense appropriations. The sequencing suggests industry is positioning for AUKUS-related procurement and regulatory decisions before FY27 budget markup.
narrative.prompt: The March 20 DSCA notification covers AUKUS-specific vertical deployment tubes and common weapon launchers for the UK at $1B. Using public DSCA letters, NDAA provisions, and congressional committee jurisdiction: which House and Senate subcommittees have oversight of AUKUS submarine industrial base funding in FY27 appropriations? How does the FMS case structure for embedded US/UK personnel differ from standard LOA procedures, and what does that imply for timeline and contractor visibility into the program?

narrative.headline: Managed money extreme wheat short suggests supply glut bet
narrative.body: CFTC COT shows managed money net short wheat at 2.8σ above baseline — a historically rare positioning extreme. With AU and CA both active on trade/agriculture lobbying, this likely reflects speculative conviction that Northern Hemisphere supply fears are overpriced.
narrative.prompt: Given CFTC managed money wheat net short at z=+2.8σ (994 contracts, week of 2026-03-24), and Manildra Milling Corporation lobbying on Trade/Agriculture/Tariffs (2026-01-19), analyze: what specific USDA or CBOT supply/demand factors would justify this short positioning in Australian and Canadian wheat export context? How have similar COT extremes in wheat resolved historically during Q1-Q2, and which Senate Agriculture subcommittee has jurisdiction over export subsidy programs that could squeeze this short?
